description:
Explorer thumbnails for 326 file types Windows can't read, plus a right-click image toolkit.

SageThumbs 2K is a modern, crash-isolated Windows 11 shell extension written in Rust that rebuilds the classic SageThumbs. It gives File Explorer thumbnails for 326 file types Windows can't preview on its own — camera RAW, HEIC/AVIF, Photoshop, DjVu, ebooks and comics, video frames, 3D-print files and a long tail of obscure formats — plus a right-click image toolkit (convert, resize, rotate, OCR, color picker, metadata strip, combine-to-PDF/CBZ) and a console/AI CLI. Free for personal use.
SageThumbs 2K 1.3.4
This patch release makes Quick preview a little faster and easier to use while tightening the
release process itself.
What changed
- Static GIF, PNG and WebP previews no longer read the same file twice while checking for
  animation.
- Large project, archive and PDF previews use bounded, preview-aware reads to avoid unnecessary
  whole-file buffering.
- Moving between files follows Explorer's natural order, so image2 comes before image10.
- The narrow preview scrollbar has a larger invisible grab area without covering more content.
- Release publishing is tied to the exact commit that passed CI, and the benchmark now reports
  real failures and percentile timings instead of accepting stale output files.
The full release test suite, dependency checks, real-format corpus and direct Quick preview smoke
tests pass.
SHA-256 (SageThumbs2K-Setup-1.3.4.exe):
